title: Cisco WLC + ISE Wireless Authentication Funnel — Association → Auth → DHCP → Posture

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.20 wireless service availability through funnel measurement.
Art.21(2)(c) business continuity — wireless service continuity.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Block DHCP for a test SSID. UC-17.1.73 shows funnel drop at DHCP phase.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Healthy onboarding. Funnel completes; no drop.

dataSources: Combined: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` (codes 5200/5400 — auth pass/fail; 5417 — accounting; posture 80001-80003) + Cisco WLC syslog `index=network sourcetype=cisco:wlc:syslog` (association events) + DHCP `index=network sourcetype=dhcp` lease events. Field common: `client_mac`, `ssid`, `ap_name`, `bssid`. Time-series correlation across the four phases.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` + `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Wireless`.
spl:
index=network sourcetype="cisco:wlc:syslog" "associated" 
| eval phase="associated"
| append [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5400) | eval phase="auth"]
| append [search index=network sourcetype="dhcp" "DHCPACK" | eval phase="dhcp"]
| append [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=80003 | eval phase="posture"]
| transaction client_mac maxspan=10m
| stats count by phase
| eval funnel_drop=phase
description: Tracks the wireless-onboarding funnel — associate → authenticate → DHCP → posture. Identifies which phase fails most for a given SSID, AP, or building.
value: Wireless onboarding spans many systems. When users complain 'I can't connect to wifi', root cause may be at any phase. The funnel quickly identifies which phase is responsible — letting operations fix the right system.
implementation: Schedule every 30 min. Requires WLC and DHCP logs in Splunk.
visualization: Sankey diagram of funnel; per-SSID drop-off heatmap; per-AP phase failure breakdown.

references.title: Cisco WLC + ISE Integration Best Practices

knownFalsePositives:
(a) Devices that never request DHCP (static-IP or VPN apps).
(b) Posture skipped for devices in posture-exception list.
mitreAttack: N/A (wireless integration — measures fast-roaming success rate, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: Connecting to wifi has 4 steps: see the network, log in, get an IP address, prove you're allowed. We watch where users drop out of this 4-step process to find which step is broken.
